label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The user wants me to figure out the best title for this passage about body language in the office. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I'll read through the passage again to make sure I understand it. The main idea is about body language in the office and what not to do. It gives specific tips like not touching co-workers, maintaining eye contact, having a good handshake, and not leaning the head while listening. The passage starts by emphasizing how powerful body language is, especially in the workplace. Now, looking at the options. Option A is "Pay attention to your body language at the office." That seems directly related because the entire passage is about what you should avoid in the office setting. Option B is about getting along with co-workers. While the passage does mention co-workers, the focus is more on body language specifically rather than general tips on getting along. So maybe B is too broad. Option C is about the meaning of body language you should know. The passage does explain meanings (like eye contact showing confidence), but the key part is the "tips on what you should not do," making it more about being cautious rather than just knowing the meanings. Option D talks about different countries having different body language. The passage doesn't mention cultural differences at all, so D is probably not
